_comment: Source of truth for OFFICIAL plugin packs in THIS repo. The release workflow reads this: it builds+uploads a pack ONLY when its `version` differs from the previous release's manifest (otherwise it carries the already-built zip forward), and attaches a public `plugins-manifest.json` to every release so the app can list available plugins + versions (the app pulls it from THIS repo's releases — no hard-coded list in the app). Plugin versions are INDEPENDENT of the app version. To ship a change: bump `version` here AND the plugin's IPlugin.Version + csproj <Version> (keep the three in sync — `node devtools/dev.mjs bump <id>` does all three). `asset` is the install contract with the app's PluginInstallService — don't rename without updating both.
_releaseVersionNote: REPO release version (the MAIN control). Bumped by the release workflow (workflow_dispatch → bump major/minor, like the app), which tags vX.Y and stamps it into the published plugins-manifest.json. SEPARATE from each plugin's own `version` below — those are bumped by the dev when a plugin changes (`dev.mjs bump <id>`, guarded by the pre-commit hook). See .claude/rules/plugin-versioning.md.

plugins.name: Content Veil AI
plugins.description: deepghs anime censor-point detector (nano ONNX, MIT) — explicit-only image review for the content veil.
plugins._versionNote: 1.1 (2026-07-12): native onnxruntime load now fails loudly (clear log + abstain, host uses heuristic) instead of a cryptic per-image crash.
